behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff contract between source collectors and the summary-input assembler: all three source payloads must be present in one combined input. This is cross-module wiring/state handoff, not just one pure function. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is required (source clients can be stubbed), no dependence on model output quality, and no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is about inclusion of retrieved fields in the handoff payload, not real infra effects or LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire collector modules with controlled stubs for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log; execute the real aggregation path; assert the object/string sent to the summary-generation interface contains all three sections with correct source-to-field mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in orchestration/error-propagation contract across modules: source failure must map to section-level fallback text while pipeline continues. This requires module interaction (collector -> report builder), not real infrastructure. Integration brake: real OS/network/subprocess is not required because an in-process failing stub can reproduce the unavailability path; correctness does not depend on model output quality; no multi-agentic sequencing. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior does not require proving real transport failure modes, only fallback handling semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with one source client stubbed to raise timeout/unavailable and the other two returning valid data; assert run completes, report includes `data unavailable` for the failed section, and no global abort/exception is emitted.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets content fidelity of generated narrative, which depends on real model judgment/output, not just deterministic plumbing. A stubbed generator would always pass and hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text requires semantic accuracy of generated summary itself, not only routing/retry/payload formatting around the call. Workflow is disqualified because only one agentic decision point (summary generation) is required, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values, call the real summary-generation model, and evaluate whether required values/claims are preserved (fact checks against source data with tolerance rules). Run repeatedly to capture nondeterministic misses and enforce an acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real infrastructure: external data APIs + email transport/inbox delivery. In-process fakes can hide auth, TLS, SMTP/API delivery, MIME formatting, and mailbox acceptance issues. Rejection gate: Agentic is disqualified as minimum because behavior text checks end-to-end delivery to inbox, not quality of model output; Workflow is disqualified because this is not defined as multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled report pipeline against real reachable endpoints (or production-like test services) and a real test mailbox; assert message is received by configured recipient, with expected subject/body/attachments and end-to-end completion status.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real infra boundaries: email service reachability failure plus durable filesystem persistence and later scheduled execution. Mocks can hide path/permission/fsync/timing/scheduler interactions that cause real retry loss. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text requires "next scheduled run" and local file durability under real runtime conditions, which exceeds in-process module wiring only. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no LLM decision quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Force real email endpoint failure (e.g., connection refused), run scheduled job, assert report is written to expected local fallback file; trigger/await next real scheduled execution with email service restored; assert previously saved report is retried and delivered, and retry state is cleared.
